text:
Market today: Broad indices are holding up (SPY +1.06%, QQQ -0.53%) but the June jobs number - only 57,000 payrolls added - is the real driver today. Weak jobs data cuts both ways: soft economy fears hurt growth/tech, but it also raises rate-cut hopes. Net effect on your chip-heavy book is cautious - not a panic, but not a green light either.

Your holdings: CRWD is screaming for attention - you're down -72.88% and your stop is listed at $590 while it's trading at $195. That stop is wildly misconfigured and not protecting you at all. SOXL is down -30.42% with a stop at $200 - it's already below that stop; you may be overdue to exit. SPAL is new, down -13.96%, and very close to its $21.38 stop on a leveraged ETF - margin amplifies any further drop fast.

Watch: Whether SOXL and SPAL breach their stops intraday. Also watch if weak jobs data accelerates selling in semis (SMH already -7.49%).

Bottom line: Your house is on fire in two or three spots - fix the stops and trim the pain before adding anything new.
plays: CRWD: Your stop at $590 is a data error - price is $195. Set a real stop now; scanner shows thin upside (R 0.67 to $196-$201). No reason to add.
SOXL: Already trading below your $200 stop at $177. Decide today - hold with a reset stop near $175 (scanner support) or exit. Leveraged decay on a margin account is brutal.
No new buys today - SPAL is near its stop, macro is shaky on weak jobs, and your existing bleeding positions need triage first.
